If you only want to look around and test, you may import DEMO data via a buttom on top of Seminary Settings.","col":12}},{"id":"w7gXrQDKLF","type":"shortcut","data":{"shortcut_name":"1. Set up how you grade (Grading Scale)","col":3}},{"id":"baMg-0VAzU","type":"shortcut","data":{"shortcut_name":"2. Tailor the Settings for your Seminary","col":3}},{"id":"MI3-vb2jT9","type":"shortcut","data":{"shortcut_name":"3. Create Academic Years (container for terms)","col":3}},{"id":"JVt5zt6Wnb","type":"shortcut","data":{"shortcut_name":"4. Create Academic Terms (and which terms are open for enrollment)","col":3}},{"id":"sDCpdWuIhV","type":"shortcut","data":{"shortcut_name":"5. Define Holidays","col":3}},{"id":"k-1XTmAlUs","type":"header","data":{"text":"Financial setup","col":12}},{"id":"iD08IvBTdA","type":"paragraph","data":{"text":"Seminary ERP is installed with some default items, except for item price. You need to input these for each price list (default selling already exists and has some items).","col":12}},{"id":"BKlddDTC9z","type":"paragraph","data":{"text":"Customer Groups can have their default price list, so you can have prices for nationals and foreigners, for instance. ","col":12}},{"id":"wy2Exgbote","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Set up your prices","col":4}},{"id":"KXmExFM4ja","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Set up how you get paid","col":4}},{"id":"KXOMQ7kHPa","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Scholarships and Instructors","col":4}},{"id":"pGwE4Umf-h","type":"header","data":{"text":"Infrastructure (optional)","col":12}},{"id":"5LZg7awNug","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Campus and Buildings","col":4}},{"id":"qGw1hawbjP","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Rooms and Features","col":4}},{"id":"rFRu1rqsAh","type":"header","data":{"text":"Academic Setup","col":12}},{"id":"E0OR-vcB9m","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"The Academic Foundation","col":4}},{"id":"SnSextXHaQ","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Graduation Requirements","col":4}},{"id":"NWnFAxcSn0","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Culminating Project Configuration","col":4}},{"id":"J-trVNnb1z","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Credit Transfer Configuration","col":4}},{"id":"K0HVd1q8Oc","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"The Academic Engine","col":4}},{"id":"FghCVmStrn","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Withdrawal and Disciplinary Management","col":4}},{"id":"MON2p0igia","type":"paragraph","data":{"text":"Each student created automatically creates a customer, but you may want to create other customers for churches who want to pay part of the tuition of their pastors, etc. Then, you can assign more payers for an individual program enrollment (e.g., student and church will be invoiced separately if you so choose). Scholarships should be used for those sponsored by the Seminary (e.g., from donations to a fund/cost-center that the seminary has freedom to manage). ","col":12}},{"id":"eN_mktrAXr","type":"shortcut","data":{"shortcut_name":"39. Customers (all)","col":3}},{"id":"IniDnIiEAG","type":"header","data":{"text":"Optional","col":12}},{"id":"jlkx0S7ghb","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Payment Gateways","col":4}},{"id":"LCUIH-mjIL","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Data Import","col":4}},{"id":"WhR1-yGjN7","type":"card","data":{"card_name":"Integrations","col":4}}]
label: Getting Started SeminaryERP

links.description: This require the installation of the app Payments and, if used for student portal, a configuration under Seminary Settings.
links.label: Payment Gateways

links.description: You can import data from another system through CSV files using this tool. You can download a template file so to organize how data must be structured for a successful import.
links.label: Data Import

links.description: When a Student is created, SeminaryERP automatically creates a user and a customer. A student can be enrolled in one or more programs, retaining the same student id.
links.label: The Academic Engine

links.description: Credit transfers are gated both here and at the program level. For your legacy system, please create a Partner Seminary using the full form.
links.label: Credit Transfer Configuration

links.description: In Fee Categories you will wire up the automation of events to charge (e.g., on course enrollment) with the Item and a Payment Term Template.
links.label: Set up how you get paid
10. Payment Terms - when you get paid
11. Fee Categories to wire it up-

links.description: Each Program may have additional requirements besides total credits and mandatory courses. These can be set here, with a common library of requirements that programs can share and per program requirements that are applied (snapshot) at program enrollment, so if graduation requirements change, it does not affect previously enrolled students.
links.label: Graduation Requirements

links.description: For each Culminating Project type, you can create a specific number of milestones (approval checkpoints). Students may have a non-determinate number of rounds in each milestone, until it is ready for approval.
links.label: Culminating Project Configuration

links.description: Scholarships are configured here and applied to students on their Program Enrollment.
links.label: Scholarships and Instructors
14. Instructor Categories for report and variable payment
** Time to check your Pricing before enrollment

links.description: This is completely optional, but helpful as the seminary has satellite locations.
links.label: Campus and Buildings

links.description: As these are populated, the registrar knows what a course requires when the room is selected for a Course Schedule (and if it meets the requirements)
links.label: Rooms and Features

name: Getting Started SeminaryERP

2. Tailor the Settings for your Seminary
4. Create Academic Terms (and which terms are open for enrollment)
